entry.title: Re: How to read a measure value into SAC Advanced Story s...
entry.content:
Hi @innokeyits 

Please try with the following code:

var data = Chart_1.getDataSource().getResultSet();
 
var topRegion = "";
var topActual = 0.00;
 
for (var i = 0; i < data.length; i++) {
   if (ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(data[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].rawValue) > topActual){
      topActual = ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(data[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].rawValue);
      topRegion = data[i]["Region"].description;
}
}
 
Input_Message.setValue("Top performing region:" + topRegion);
 
 
You can read the measure value with the [Alias.MeasureDimension]. There you can decide whether you want the Raw or the Formatted value. I prefer to work with the raw value as I had issues in the past with the formatted value.
 
I hope this helps.
 
Kind regards
Viktor

entry.title: How SAP Fuels Human Insights and AI Innovation with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content: At SAP, we're turning business data into a smart, semantically enriched asset - fueling both human insight and AI innovation with SAP Business Data Cloud. Join this session to see how we're building a powerful data product architecture which is accelerating SAP’s data-driven AI first transformation.

entry.title: New Pipeline for Cloud Integration features with version 1.0.11
entry.content:
A new version 1.0.11 of the Process Integration Pipeline package has been recently shipped, this time with rather minor changes but two changes require your attention because here we decided to change default configurations to follow our recommendations. If you nevertheless like to use the old default settings, you need to configure your integration flows before deployment.

So, here the list of new features/changes at a glance:

  • The search based on IDoc number in the message monitor has been improved by removing the leading zeros from IDoc number in ApplicationID and custom header properties.
  • For the XI inbound flow, the partner ID is determined also based on service interface namespace in addition to system name and service interface name.
  • We fixed an error with wrong inbound queue in case of custom error handling for the integrated messaging runtime.
  • We introduced a configuration option to switch off the option to determine the partner ID using the so called "tilde option". The default is that this option is switched off.
  • We changed the default inbound processing queue of the XI inbound and the IDoc inbound flows from PIPQ01 to PIPX01 so that by default the integrated messaging runtime is used.

Here some more details:

Switch off "tilde option"

The partner ID unambiguously defines the configuration scenarios and is usually based on the sender system name and the sender interface name. The recommended approach is to define the partner ID as configuration scenario name and then to map sender system name and the sender interface name to the partner ID using an alternative partner.

An alternative option is to define the partner ID by combining the sender system name and the sender interface name separated by a tilde, hence I call it "tilde option". This was the only option when we shipped the first version of the pipeline concept and so we still support it for compatibility reasons.

We do not recommend to use the "tilde option" because with this you may exceed the maximum length of the partner ID in the Partner Directory which is 60 characters. So, we came up with the option defining alternative partners. This also has the advantage that it easily supports sender wildcard scenarios, so different sender systems with same sender interface sharing the same configuration. Here, you simply add another alternative partner to the configuration scenario.

In a first attempt, the partner ID is determined based on an alternative partner matching the headers SAP_Sender and SAP_SenderInterface which are passed from the scenario-specific inbound flows. So far, if no partner ID could be determined, in a second attempt a partner ID was constructed by combining the two headers separated by tilde assuming that this option should be used.

If you however did not intend to use the "tilde option" and for whatever reason the partner ID could not be determined, e.g., because you missed to maintain the alternative partner, the partner ID was unintentionally constructed in a wrong way. The message most likely went into an error because the constructed partner ID did not exist in the Partner Directory. The message was not lost because it stayed on the first JMS queue in the pipeline, but the issue was that there was no way that you could change the partner ID header of the message to get the message restarted with a correct partner ID.

To avoid such a situation, we introduced an externalized parameter CustomXPid_SwitchOffTilde. The default value is true, so the "tilde option" is switched off. The new behaviour is that if we could not determine the partner ID based on an alternative partner, we do not automatically construct the partner ID and the message rather goes into an error. The difference is that you are now able to resolve the error resolution by fixing the Partner Directory entry and restarting the message.

We intentionally decided to set the default to true which unfortunately is an incompatible change if you like to use the "tilde option". For those of you who prefer to use the "tilde option", you need to take the following action before deploying the generic flows of the updated package: Select the following generic flows where the script to determine the partner ID is called and for each flow change the value of the externalized parameter CustomXPid_SwitchOffTilde from true to false:

  • Pipeline Generic Step01 - Inbound Processing for Idoc
  • Pipeline Generic Step01 - Inbound Processing for XI
  • Pipeline Generic Step02 - Inbound Processing
  • Pipeline Generic Step02 - Integrated Messaging Runtime Async
  • Pipeline Generic Step02 - Integrated Messaging Runtime Sync

No action required when you like to stick to our recommendation using alternative partner.

Figure 1: Tilde option vs alternative partner

 

Default inbound processing queue

For each scenario-specific flow, you explixitly define the inbound processing queue where you store the incoming message. Depending on the queue chosen, the message is either processed within the fully decoupled pipeline or the integrated messaging runtime. If you keep the JMS queue prefix to the default value which is PIP, PIPQ01 stands for the fully decoupled pipeline whereas messages that are stored in PIPX01 are picked up by the integrated messaging runtime.

The fully decoupled pipeline consists of separate integration flows for each pipeline step so that you end up at 4 JMS queues required to decouple the pipeline steps. Furthermore, for a recipient list pattern with additional interface determination it requires an XSLT for the receiver determination and for each receiver an XSLT for the interface determination.

The integrated messaging runtime has been evolved from the fully decoupled pipeline by combining several pipeline steps into one integration flow. Here, you only need 2 JMS queues and only one single XSLT mapping per scenario to determine receivers and interfaces.

Recommendation is to use the integrated messaging runtime for reasons of better tenant resource consumption and better runtime behaviour due to less number of logs, less number of JMS queues and less number of XSLT mappings. The only scenarios that are not supported on the integrated messaging runtime are those where you would like to reuse extended receiver determination because here you separate the receiver determination from the interface determination, however the integrated messaging runtime only supports a combined XSLT. 

The generic XI inbound flow and the generic IDoc inbound flow are used across multiple scenarios and hence the inbound processing queue is generally defined across multiple scenarios. If you like to define an inbound queue for a particular scenario, you need to configure the string parameter inboundQueue in the Partner Directory to overwrite the default value for this particular scenario. For compatibility reasons, so far the default inbound processing queue was configured as PIPQ01. In order to follow our recommendation to use the integrated messaging runtime instead of the fully decoupled pipeline, we decided to change the default from PIPQ01 to PIPX01.

So, with this change the XI and IDoc messages are now routed to the integrated messaging runtime by default, and if you like to route the message for a particular scenario to the fully decoupled pipeline you need to create a string parameter inboundQueue with value PIPQ01.

If you however like to stick to the old behaviour, you need to take action before deploying the two generic flows: Select the following generic flows and for each flow change the value of the externalized parameter DefaultJMSQueueNumber from X01 to Q01:

  • Pipeline Generic Step01 - Inbound Processing for XI
  • Pipeline Generic Step01 - Inbound Processing for Idoc

No action required when you like to stick to our recommendation using X01.

 

Partner ID for XI inbound

For ABAP proxy scenarios, there might be situations where the combination of sender system name and sender interface name alone is not unambiguous, namely if you have a scenario with the same sender system name and the same sender interface name but different sender system namespaces which is actually supported in SAP Process Orchestration. For this reason, the approach determining the partner ID has been extended for the XI inbound flow. If you like to leverage the new feature, you need to define the alternative partner in your configuration scenario differently:

  • The agent remains the same, here you maintain the sender system name.
  • The scheme was constant before, now you need to maintain the sender interface name.
  • And for the ID, you maintain the sender interface namespace.

When matching the alternative partner to a partner ID, in a first attempt such a combination incl. namespace is checked. If we do not have a match, then in a second attempt the usual approach without namespace is applied.

Figure 2: Determine partner ID based on sender system name, sender interface name and sender interface namespace

entry.title: SAP DM - Update downtime timestamp with downtime API
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Use Case - we have a requirement where we need to capture the resource downtime in SAP DM using automation. 

Solution - we have thought to use the downtime API provided by SAP DM - Link But the problem we are facing is that we don't have the PATCH method to update the end downtime. Only POST(CREATE) method is provided by SAP DM.

Ask: have anyone came across this scenario where we need to log downtime using API and later on once resource in production we have updated the end time of the downtime.

Please note that we can't use POD's as this will be directly coming from the shopfloor based on that we have to make resource up/down.

Please let me know if there is any workaround of it as per our understanding update method is very much needed to update the same details in downtime table.

Quick help will be appreciated.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: Quedada SAP - post SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content: Cool! Looking forward to it!!

entry.title: Re: Rebate to Payer capped on a Ship-to Customer
entry.content:
Thanks @MichaelWerner for the response. We need a single settlement document per month.

The cap per customer is for the entire year. This means that if the cap is $1000, in the first month if $700 is paid, the next month the rebate should be only be restricted at $300 (1000-700)

Is this possible with the routine and the settlement split at ship-to party level?

The Group Key Routine which you are referring is that the  Group Condition Routine in the condition type configuration or am i  missing something.

entry.title: When reversing MM Document, Check the original document's posting date.
entry.content:
Hi,

In our business, when a MM dcoument is reversed, the posting date of the reversed document must be made in the same month as the original document.

In order to make users follow these rules, I would like to implement the validity check logic for the posting date of the reversed document using the custom logic of the public cloud.

Which business context of the custom logic in public cloud can be used to implement it?

entry.title: Re: Materials to learn SAP Cloud Indentity Services, IAS,...
entry.content:
Hi Margit,

Thanks for such a detailed comment for anyone to understand and follow the roadmap!
You always ensure that the queries are addressed in the most righteous way!
Glad to have found you here in the forum!
Hoping for a long healthy association with you.
Thanks a lot in advance for everything.
Best Regards
Arun

entry.title: How to Create a custom report to display whether the document number contains the attachment or not
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement in an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 2-system landscape. The client needs a report that displays whether a journal entry document number has an attachment or not.

Additionally, I would like to know where the attachment is stored when a document is uploaded through the "Manage Journal Entries" app.

If anyone has insights on how to achieve this, please let me know.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: MaxDB dbanalyser dbanalyser_InstanceParameterCheck In...
entry.content: HI HP, the new version of dbanalyzer_instanceParameterCheck.cfg is available now. Regards Birgit Malik IMS SAP MaxDB

entry.title: Re: Publishing to a Queue or Topic in BTP Event Mesh usin...
entry.content:
Hi @hjshin,

from the screenshots that you've provided, I can see that your queue is named: 

sap/demo/ai/messages

 You need to URL encode that full queue name when sending messages via the REST API:

https://<http rest address>/messagingrest/v1/queues/<queueUrlEncoded>/messages
https://<http rest address>/messagingrest/v1/queues/sap%2Fdemo%2Fai%2Fmessages/messages

 You could also send a message to the topic via the REST API:

https://<http rest address>/messagingrest/v1/topics/<topicUrlEncoded>/messages
https://<http rest address>/messagingrest/v1/topics/sap%2Fdemo%2Fai%2Fevent%2Fscraping%2Frequested/messages

I hope this clarifies your question.

Best regards,
Tobias

entry.title: Re: MaxDB dbanalyser dbanalyser_InstanceParameterCheck In...
entry.content: is available now - Regards Birgit Malik IMS SAP MaxDB

But how to handle that data into receiver system? is there some standard FM/BAPI/etc which we can use as a process code? or we have to create a custom process code to directly map the segment fields to it's respective tables?

entry.title: Re: SAP SNOW INTEGARATION INBOUND IDOC using CREMAS Msg.t...
entry.content: Thanks, @helmut_skolaut3. But in my case, I need to use STATUS message type.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere CLI not working
entry.content:
Hello, 

Correct command is: datasphere config cache init --host "<url>"

Refer to the official help for command syntaxes: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/d0ecd6f297ac40249072a44df0549c1a/d483a0f480af452e9dda47f885ab87ad.html?locale=en-US.

You can also use the built-in help with: datasphere <command> --help --host "<url>".

Here are the most commons errors and their solutions: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/faq-amp-troubleshooting-guide-for-sap-datasphere-cli/ba-p/13550029.

Best regards,

Clément

entry.title: Guideline to analyze any existing custom transactions to move Fiori App or Embedded Analytics
entry.content:
Migrating SAP Custom Reports to Fiori or SAC: A Step-by-Step Guide for SAP Developers
As organizations continue to evolve, the need to modernize SAP custom reports has become increasingly important. The adoption of SAP Fiori and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) offers a significant opportunity to enhance business intelligence and user experience.
This guide is designed to provide SAP developers with a structured approach to analyzing and migrating custom reports to these modern platforms. The following steps will outline the key considerations and best practices for a successful migration.
We need perform below activity for identification:

Step 1:  Assessment of T-Code

Identify Report Property: Get the report name, program name, program type (report, module pool.), author, user and data sources
Categorized: Identify Business use and core module example: Sales reports, financial reports…etc.
Check the complexity of the program
Step 2: Analyze Report Usage

Check the uses and frequency using SAP workload analysis example ST06N or STAD
Step 3: Technical Analysis

Data Sources: Identify the data sources used by report example tables, view, function modulus, CDS views…etc. This is essential for determine the feasibility of migration to Fiori or SAC.


Report complexity: check the complexity of the report logics example simple read and write or complex logics changing the business documents example sales order, purchase order…etc.


Output Format: Analyze the output format of the report example ALV Grid, classic list, custom module pool screen or smart forms…etc.
  

Performance Analysis: Check the performance of reports using trace enable execution via ST05 or performance check. Identify Key ABAP constructs like SELECT statements, LOOPs, AT NEW Statements and any custom function module used.


Data retrieval logic: Analyze how data fetched form database, are there any complex joins, sub-queries, nested select statements these may need optimization when moving Fiori/SAC


Event Handling: Check any interactive element in the reports example user input, drill downs.


Check the Fiori Suitability: If report consist of data entry or modification can be easily migrated to Fiori app.


For analytical reports: report with interactive charts, graphs and drill-down functionality can be migrated to Fiori using Analytical list Pages (ALPs) or Overview (OVP)


Check the SAC Suitability: Reports contain analytical report and dashboard, complex calculations, aggregation and visualization are ideal for SAC.


Check the data visualization if report contains advanced data visualization techniques choose SAC


Check the report are used for planning and forecasting then use SAC.


Check Report if highly customized or unusual requirement that are replicate in Fiori/SAC may not compatible for migration


 Check the Data Modeling CDS VDM is optimized for performance


 Check the security model for authorization models.


Step 4: Flow Chart

Check existing report and design flow chart diagram for report functionality
Step 5: Documentation

Perform the documentation for migration process.

In conclusion, migrating SAP custom reports to Fiori or SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) requires a thorough assessment and analysis of the existing reports. By following the five-step process outlined above, SAP developers can effectively evaluate the feasibility of migration and determine the best approach for each report.

entry.title: BP is not creating with respect to new customer creation
entry.content:
I used the class CMD_EI_API and the method MAINTAIN_DIRECT_INPUT to pass customer data into the deep structure. It successfully updated the customer in table KNA1, but the Business Partner (BP) was not created. I checked the BP records and confirmed it wasn’t generated. Please let me know how to resolve this

   CALL METHOD cmd_ei_api=>maintain_direct_input
      EXPORTING
        iv_collect_messages  = gc_x
        is_master_data       = ls_customer
      IMPORTING
        es_message_defective = ls_error.

entry.title: Configuration ID "AGS_USER_BATCH" at TAC SOLMAN_SETUP_ADMIN, Generic Storage Admin
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I hope you are doing well.

LMDB background job SAP_LMDB_LDB_000000001 is failing with error message "An exception of class CX_LMDB_USER_ERROR occurred, Cannot retrieve batch user; check transaction SOLMAN_SETUP".

I have referred to the SAP Note 2530126 - LMDB background job SAP_LMDB_LDB_000000001 is failing and checked the Configuration ID "AGS_USER_BATCH" at TAC SOLMAN_SETUP_ADMIN, Generic Storage Admin.

I have only one entry with correct client and SID was incorrect. it should test system SID TTM, but it is having production SID PTM. So, I have deleted the Configuration ID "AGS_USER_BATCH" at TAC SOLMAN_SETUP_ADMIN, Generic Storage Admin in Test system (TTM).

Kindly guide me to regenerate this Configuration ID "AGS_USER_BATCH" with SID TTM.

Your guidance on this would be much appreciated.

Regards

Srinivas V

entry.title: Re: SEN and S/4 sso issue X-Frame-Options
entry.content: Thank you Shane for your answer. The sso for SEN was setup by SAP using OpenID to our IAS tenant. That is the same tenant as where S4 is connected to. S4 is using Saml for sso

entry.title: Re: How to use two destinations in SAP ui5 app
entry.content: { "source": "^/odata/(.*)$", "target": "/odata/$1", "destination": "SFSF_WZ_TECH_START_RECRUIT", "authenticationType": "xsuaa", "csrfProtection": false },

entry.title: Re: Distribution of customer to lower environments. Produ...
entry.content:
Hello @Akhil6623 

The inbound process code for DEBMAS messages is DEBM.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Hello All, How to delete the SFC with leading space from ME?
entry.content:
User created SFC with leading space in ME that we want to remove. I tried to delete using Delete/unscrap functionality but it throws error 'SFC, process lot, or shop order D0xxxxx does not exist (Message 13008)'.

Is there any way to delete this SFC?

entry.title: Re: Automatic clearing customer open items based on FIFO...
entry.content:
Hi @Linhv ,

Yes, the FIFO processing instruction is available as a valid action of processing rules in the Manage Processing Rules app. 

For more detailed information, I recommend you to have a look at the following help content and to follow all the help links from there: Overview on All Predelivered Processing Instructions & How to Create Processing Rules

Best regards,
Anita

entry.title: Re: Column editable on Fiori Element List report
entry.content: Hi @Chamdeep557 , I understand that but in my case this is not getting triggered as once I click the edit button, the call stack goes in edit press function and unable to get this.oTable value despite giving correct Id in OnInit function. Also my debugger is not stopping in onInit function. Kindly help and suggest, what am I doing wrong.

entry.title: Does SAP JCo hold memory if all heavy logic is in ABAP and Java just waits for the result?
entry.content:
I'm using SAP Java Connector (JCo 3.x) to call an ABAP remote-enabled function module (FM) from a Java application.

The scenario:

  • The FM performs heavy processing entirely inside ABAP
  • Importing/exporting parameters are small (just a few scalars and short messages)
  • Java uses JCoDestinationManager.getDestination() and calls .execute()
  • I do not use JCoContext.begin() — so it's a stateless call

The issue:

  • Despite minimal input/output, our Java process shows large memory spikes
  • If two JCo calls run concurrently, total memory usage reaches 8 GB and crashes
  • ABAP logic is confirmed to be memory-heavy (large SELECTs, joins, etc.), but it completes normally when called standalone

Question:

  • Does JCo hold backend session data or native memory while waiting for the ABAP FM to complete?
  • Can memory usage grow on the Java side just because of ABAP-side memory, even when nothing large is returned?
  • What is the best way to avoid memory pressure in this scenario?

Any clarification on JCo behavior or best practices is appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Access Control - Assessment of users, roles and their...
entry.content:
Hello RafaMarj,

in my opinion there is a significant risk involved in having another company on the same system.

For example it ist probably still possible to create an access request involving a role of the other company which would grant authorizations to a system they shouldn't have access to. Even if the normal user isn't able to request these roles there certainly has to be at least one administrator who has this option.

Furthermore this constellation imposes that the different systems are sharing the same IT-environment which leads to more risks regarding hacking, mistakes, firewall problems and so on.

I would not recommend this approach. If you still chose to use it I would at least speak with your SAP Basis team, IT-infrastructe team and legal department.

Regards

entry.title: Is SAP providing any report to change support-team for a closed Change Request/Change Document
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I hope you are doing well.

I had few closed Change Requests/Change Documents, where selected support team group was incorrect.

I need to change the support team group id to correct/right support team group id.

As the Change Requests/Change Documents were already in closed status. It was not allowing me to change directly from the Change Requests/Change Documents.

SAP provides a report (e.g. AI_SDK_SP_AUTO_CLOSE) to change status in a ticket. Is SAP providing a similar report to change support-team?

Your guidance on this would be much appreciated.

Regards

Srinivas V

entry.title: Re: Operation Subcontracting in Production Order
entry.content:
Generally speaking, in the production process of production orders, the WIP products have no material numbers and are not subject to inventory management.

For Subcontracting Operation, there are also no material numbers, and inventory management is not carried out either.

If inventory management is to be carried out for WIP products, it may be necessary to establish a separate semi-finished product material and create a production order for it.

For Subcontracting, if inventory management is desired, it is recommended to create an F30 material and execute the procurement Subcontracting process, thank you!

entry.title: Re: Duplicated Batch Item Cost
entry.content:
Hi,

This is standard system behaviour, if you are using the same batch number it will recalculate the current cost. If you want to have a separate cost for each receipt, you need to use separate batch numbers.

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 how to filter at column level
entry.content: it worked. appreciate your help

entry.title: Re: This document could not be opened error CR 2020
entry.content:
Hi,

can you please post some more info? What error are you getting, what is going on (program crashing, freezing, something else?).

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Participate in the Documentation Feedback Survey for SAP Job Scheduling Service
entry.content:
Are you using the SAP Job Scheduling service? Have you ever had a hard time understanding the concepts and processes described in the documentation on SAP Help Portal?

Your insights are invaluable to us! We invite you to participate in our Documentation Feedback Survey, available at https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling. Your feedback will help us enhance the quality and usability of our documentation, ensuring it meets your needs and expectations.

 

Why Your Feedback Matters

Documentation is a crucial resource for users navigating the SAP Job Scheduling service. It provides guidance, troubleshooting tips, and detailed explanations of features. However, we understand that documentation can always be improved. Your feedback will help us identify areas that need clarity, additional examples, or even new sections.

 

How to Participate

1. Head over to https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling.

2. Choose Give Feedback.

3. Share your thoughts with us. Let us know what you find helpful, what could be improved, and any specific suggestions you have.

Your input directly influences the updates and enhancements we make to our documentation.

 

What We Aim to Achieve

By gathering feedback from you, we aim to create documentation that is not only informative but also intuitive and user-friendly. Whether you're a seasoned SAP user or new to job scheduling, your perspective is crucial in shaping the resources we provide.

 

Thank you for taking the time to help us improve. Your participation is a step towards better, more effective documentation for everyone. We look forward to hearing from you!

entry.title: Re: How to Keep MEMBERSET Scope in DATA Function
entry.content:
Dear @ShrutiN, 

the MEMBERSET restricts the data which is read. 
This means you need to restrict your data by using the SD_PRODUCT property COMPANYCODE, like follows:

MEMBERSET [d/SD_PRODUCT].[p/COMPANYCODE] = ("100", "101", "102", "103", "104", "105", "106", "107", "108")

DATA([d/SD_PRODUCT] = [d/SD_PRODUCT].[p/ID], [d/COMP_CODE] = [d/SD_PRODUCT].[p/COMPANYCODE], [d/PRODUCT] = [d/SD_PRODUCT].[p/PRODUCT]) 
= RESULTLOOKUP([d/SD_PRODUCT] = "#", [d/PRODUCT] = [d/SD_PRODUCT].[p/PRODUCT], [d/COMP_CODE] = [d/SD_PRODUCT].[p/COMPANYCODE])

I hope this helps.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: This document could not be opened error CR 2020
entry.content: The error is 'This document could not be opened' the application is working and other documents I've created work normally, but I have several with the same issue.

entry.title: App could't be opened either due to an incorrect configuration or a missing role assignment.
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I am trying to upload Customer Returns through Import Sales Documents. However, I am getting the below error message. which is the newly available 2502.2 version. However system showing me the error like this below: 

"The navigation target "#CustomerReturn-import?sap-app-origin-hint=" of the app could not be resolved. This can be caused, for example, by an incorrect SAP Fiori launchpad content configuration or an incorrect or missing role assignment.''

We have checked that all the relevant roles are assigned in the user ID, along with Configurartion is fine. Need to know the case; what actions are required additionally to upload the Import Customer Returns File?

We are appreciating your response. If you know, how could I resolve it? 

Please look into my below screenshot:

 

 Thank Ypu
 Hasnat

entry.title: Re: How do I make a field from a child entity available a...
entry.content: If you remove the position, it just defaults to the last entry on display. Hidden and exclude hide the field from both the display and the configuration, so the field is not available at all. Visible is not a valid annotation.

entry.title: Receive PAIN format file from S4 to CPI when a payment is triggered
entry.content:
Hi All

We are currently working on triggering payments from S/4HANA public cloud to SAP CPI and aim to receive the output  in PAIN XML format.

We have already activated scope item 2YM and maintained the communication arrangement SAP_COM_0256 outbound services, However, we are facing challenges in identifying the correct service path to configure, as the relevant service appears deprecated in the SAP API Hub, preventing us from verifying the endpoint details.

We attempted to use the paymentadvicenotification service, which is functioning correctly, but the output does not come in PAIN XML format. We also tried configuring the paths for paymentmedia and DMEFileprocessing, but both resulted in a 404 not found error.

Could anyone please advise on the correct service path or configuration steps required to receive the PAIN format file via CPI?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Generation repository objects ADT
entry.content: @Felipe_Borba, can you provide more insight on your error? Maybe screenshots on what the error says.

entry.title: Re: Error Message FZ 376 : Error in withholding tax calculation S/4HANA
entry.content: You could add a SAP managed tagh related to your editin, S/4HANA premise or cloud, private or public

entry.title: Re: How to Create a custom report to display whether the...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

There’s no out-of-the-box report that tells you this directly. But it’s possible to build one using custom development. The basic idea is:

1. Get the journal entry list : you can use the standard CDS view I_JournalEntry or I_JournalEntryItem for this.

2. Check attachments : you’ll need to call the Attachment Service API (API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV) to check if an attachment exists for each document.

 

This kind of setup typically requires a small custom app or a report where you loop through journal entries and call the API to check for attachments. You can then show a simple Yes/No flag for each entry.

If you're working purely within the key user tools in S/4HANA Cloud, you might be limited — you'd likely need help from a developer to build this with custom logic or via a side-by-side app in BTP.

Here’s a link Help SAP that can tell you more about the Attachment Service API.

 

Summary

  • No standard report exists, but you can build one using a custom CDS view and the Attachment API.
  • Attachments are stored in the SAP backend (DMS) and linked via the BKPF object type.
  • You’ll need the API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV service to check for attachment presence.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: FSM Business Rule variable - assign fields depending...
entry.content:
Hi André

For the variables section, Javascript goes inside the curly braces. The outside is CoreSQL.

Have you tried

equiBez = ${equipment.udf.EQ_ITOB_INVNR ?? equipment.code}

Best regards

    Raphael Barabas

entry.title: Re: New Review Order Progress - Multilevel View App
entry.content:
@SAP  Hello, this is strange behavior. Can you please raise a ticket, we need to investigate this. Thanks and best regards, 

Markus

entry.title: How to Monitor Adobe Document Services (ADS) Request Usage in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

According to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Service Use Description, each customer tenant is entitled to 2,000,000 ADS API requests per year for form generation via Adobe Document Services (ADS).
We are using standard PDF form outputs (e.g. billing documents, purchase orders), and I understand that each form generation counts as one request.

However, I couldn’t find a way to monitor or track the number of ADS requests made in our tenant.

Does SAP provide any tool, dashboard, API, or log that can help:

  • track the current number of ADS API calls used,

  • estimate remaining quota for the year,

  • or identify high-usage documents or processes?

Thanks in advance!

Adrian

entry.title: Re: sapbusinessonesdk.dll error after updgrade from sap b...
entry.content:
I resolve it by deleting Reference for SAPBusinessOneSDK.DLL and adding two references to SAPbobsCOM and SAPbouiCOM

Thanks

entry.title: Automatic Stock Transfer Requisition Based on ATP Check Across Multiple Plants (Diff. Company Codes)
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently testing stock transfer orders between different plants in S4/HANA Cloud Public Edition: 1010, 1710, and 3310. Each of these plants belongs to a different company code (1010, 1710, and 3310 respectively). all the Org units are on the same Cloud Public Edition System.

Our desired scenario is as follows:

When a customer places an order in plant 1010 (Germany) for material TG00, a sales order is created. During this process, we would like the system to automatically check material availability (ATP) in plants 1710 and 3310. If stock is available in either of those plants, the system should automatically create a stock transfer purchase requisition from one or two of the supplying plant (1710 or 3310) to plant 1010.

We have the following questions:

  1. How should we configure the system to define plants 1710 and 3310 as possible supplying plants for material TG00? Should we use Purchasing Info Records, a Special Procurement Key, or another method?

  2. How can we set up the ATP check to consider stock availability in both plants 1710 and 3310 for the requested delivery date in the sales order?

We are currently testing the Best Practices BME, BMH, 1P7, 5HP

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

Feel free to reply for further queries. If it helped, mark thread as accepted solution.
Thanks & Regards,
Kunal Bansal, SAP CX SME
https://www.linkedin.com/in/thekunalbansal

In your case, you were #28 to register for the event, meaning you will be on the waitlist.

I need to change the long text on thousands of articles but can't find where I do this. Unfortunately I have limited authorizations in SAP so I don't have access to all TCodes 😞

I have checked TCode MM17 but can only find what I think is from screen field SKTEXT-MAKTX

Any suggestions from you experts?

kind regards

Tina

Before putting my question I had gone through several SAP blogs and such kind of scenarios are found in this link, please go through it -- https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/pp-subcontracting-with-one-operation-external/qaq-p/9336071

entry.title: Re: How to read a measure value into SAC Advanced Story s...
entry.content:
Hi Please do below

var data = Chart.getDataSource().getResultSet()	;
var Gross = data[0]['@MeasureDimension'].formattedValue;
Input_Message.setValue(data[0]["Location_4nm2e04531"].description);
for (var i = 0; i < data.length; i++) {
 
var v2 = data[i]['@MeasureDimension'].formattedValue;	
 
if ( Number.parseInt(Gross) < Number.parseInt(v2) ) {
	 console.log(Gross);
	Gross = v2;
	 console.log(Gross);
var v3 = data[i]["Location_4nm2e04531"].description;
console.log(v3);
	Input_Message.setValue(data[i]["Location_4nm2e04531"].description);
 
}}

KantriceD

entry.title: Re: BP is not creating with respect to new customer creation
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Customer Master Data (KNA1) and Business Partner (BP) are tightly integrated, and the Business Partner is the leading object.

Therefore, when working with S/4HANA Cloud (particularly the Public Edition), you must use the Business Partner API or interfaces to create/update customer data not the older direct customer-related APIs like CMD_EI_API.

 

Why your approach didn't create the BP

- CMD_EI_API=>MAINTAIN_DIRECT_INPUT is part of the classic customer creation APIs, typically used in ECC systems.

- In S/4HANA, using this class might still update legacy tables like KNA1, but it does not trigger the necessary synchronization with the Business Partner framework, so the BP is not created or updated.

- SAP has moved to a Business Partner-centric model where customer/vendor roles are added to a BP.

 

Correct Approach for S/4HANA Cloud (especially Public Edition)

To correctly create or update a customer, including the BP:

Use the Business Partner API:

- API Name: API_BUSINESS_PARTNER

- Service Name: API_BUSINESS_PARTNER (OData V2)

You can use the service to:

    • Create Business Partner
    • Add Customer role (FLCustomer)
    • Maintain addresses, company code, sales area data, etc...

 

Example Steps (via API or BAdI)

  1. Create Business Partner with general data
  2. Assign Customer Role (FLCustomer) to BP
  3. Add Company Code & Sales Area Data

This ensures the customer is correctly created in both KNA1 and the BP framework.

 

Alternatives (if working within SAP-internal logic or extensions):

- Use BAdIs or custom logic with the standard BP API or CDS views

- Use the Business Partner replication framework if integrated with external systems (like MDG or non-SAP CRM)

 

Here’s a summary

Do not use CMD_EI_API in S/4HANA Cloud for customer creation. Instead, use the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER service to ensure proper creation of both the Business Partner and associated customer roles.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: System Access
entry.content:
Hello Omar-Javaid, 

the business demo users (eg. S4H_FIN_DEM and others) usually are lacking the credentials for such system changes indeed.

You can log on with DDIC/SAP* or BPINST and use those to assign further authorizations or make the changes directly. 
As usual, please be aware that if you change fundamental system properties, you might face problems in running the system.  

Best, 

Joerg

entry.title: SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 + ESM APIs = ETag header for client-side HTTP caching
entry.content:
This blog article is primarily aimed at developers who are looking to enhance their understanding of the ETag and If-Match headers. By ensuring the correct implementation of these headers, developers can effectively update field values in SAP Sales Cloud and Service Cloud & ESM APIs. This approach not only guarantees data integrity but also helps reduce the high number of concurrent API usages, leading to improved performance and reliability.

ETags & If-Match

  1. The Developer makes a GET request to the CNS server.
  2. The server responds with a 200 OK status, including the content requested and an ETag header.
  3. The client caches the response and the ETag value.
  4. For subsequent requests to the same resource, the client includes the If-Match header with the ETag value it has cached while updating the field value or the record.
  5. The server regenerates the ETag independently and checks if the ETag value sent by the client matches the generated one.
    • If they match, the server responds with a 200 OK  Modified status, indicating that the client’s cached version is valid, and the field is updated with request body is sent in PATCH request.
    • If they don’t match, the server responds with a 412 Precondition Failed status, indicating that the request conditions were not met or 428 status, Request sent is incorrect

How If-Match works with ETag to prevent mid-air collisions during simultaneous updates.

Explanation:

  • Client1 and Client2 both make GET requests to the server and receive the same ETag value ("12345").
  • Both clients cache the response along with the ETag value.
  • When both clients attempt to update the resource simultaneously, they include the If-Match header with the cached ETag value.
  • The server regenerates the ETag independently for each request.
    • If the ETag matches, the server updates the resource and responds with a 200 OK status.
    • If the ETag does not match (due to another update), the server responds with a 412 Precondition Failed status, preventing the update and ensuring data integrity.

This mechanism helps prevent mid-air collisions by ensuring that only one client can successfully update the resource if the ETag matches, while others receive a failure response if the resource has been modified by another client.

Practical examples of using If-Match to ensure data integrity and consistency

Example 1 : How to get ETag value using GET Method - Response Header contains "Last Modified Date"
Example 2 : How to pass ETag value to PATCH method to update the field - Response Header Matches and updates the field value with Response 200 OK
Example 3 : How to pass ETag value to PATCH method to update the field with Incorrect If Match - Response Header validates against ETag and If-Match - If not Matched Response with 412 is sent
Example 4 : How to pass ETag value to PATCH method to update the field with Incorrect Header - Response Header validates against ETag and If-Match - If Header is incorrect 428 is sent

A few key points to keep in mind:

  • Always wrap your ETag values in double quotes when sending them

  • If the client is a performing in browser (SSC V2 UI), it’ll automatically manage the cache and send conditional requests without any extra work.

 

References

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Headers/ETag

entry.title: Re: How to Monitor Adobe Document Services (ADS) Request...
entry.content:
Hello,

In the on-premise environment, there is a related note: 3591841 - How to monitor number of ADS calls - onprem.
The report FP_ADS_PERF_LOG_ANALYZER mentioned in this note comes from note 3519313, and it turns out that this note can also be applied in the PCE environment, and the report runs successfully.

Would you like to take a look at the note?

Best Regards

Park sangyong

entry.title: Generating Billing Document Request from Service Contract
entry.content:
I’m using SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud Edition and currently working on generating a Billing Document Request (BDR) from a service contract. When the contract contains only one line item, the system creates a single BDR as expected. However, when I include multiple line items in the service contract—even if all of them share the same billing date—the system generates separate BDRs for each line item. My requirement is to generate a single BDR that includes all line items corresponding to that billing date. Could someone please guide me on how to achieve this?

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP CPI is not picking few files from Sender SFTP AL11.
entry.content: Daily around 40 files will be placed in AL11 in one of the directory and initially due to some issue with 9 files the files went to processing mode in SAP CPI for 24 hrs and they we're discarded. Since then those file names are not being picked up by CPI, we tried to change the file name they we're successful but I need the exact file names to be processed from the same directory (client requirement) and post processing they should be placed in archive folder. Is there any solution to it, Receiver is also SFTP and we're reading the file name from property and on receiver side the filename is called through property so I cannot append timestamp. client requirement is the filename should be same daily so that they should overwrite the existing file daily. Below is the SFTP detail for reference.

entry.title: Is it possible to see the reason for variance for scrap or rework in manage production order app?
entry.content:
When using app (ID F2335), Manage Production Order, is it possible to display the reason for scrap or rework?

When hoovering over the bar chart, there is a popup information box, showing the number for rework (or scrap) Is there a way to show the reason here as well? Many thanks Adrian

entry.title: How to administrate change documents?
entry.content:
The below Change Document data needs to be retained/archived:

User Change Data:

  • Change history for logon data - USH02
  • Change history for authorizations - USH04
  • Change history for authorization profiles - USH10
  • Change history for authorization values - USH12

 

Role Change Data:

  • CDHDR
  • CDPOS


Kindly advise on how the data can be transferred as CDHDR, CDPOS also contains Change logs from another table as well.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to administrate change documents?
entry.content:
Please read the below contents carefully regarding the administration of Change Documents:

  • SAP Note 419933 - FAQ: Maintenance of change documents in user administration
  • KBA 3015823 - CD: Archiving change documents - How To

entry.title: Creating Conditions for Alternative Order Units in Info Records
entry.content:
I need to create or update purchasing info records along with their associated conditions. I'm currently using the function module ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI, which works well in most cases. However, in some situations, I need to add a condition for an alternative order unit (UoM).

 

From what I’ve observed, conditions are stored in table A067, and each condition entry generates a new KNUMH (condition record number) for the corresponding order unit (BSTME).

The issue is that this FM always applies the condition based on the EINA-BSTME (standard order unit) field. I haven't found a way to assign a condition to a different UoM.

I've also tried the following function modules, but they didn’t fulfill the requirement:

  • CND_PRICES_DETAILS_SAVE

  • ME_UPDATE_INFORECORD_COND

Does anyone know of a function module or method that allows conditions to be created for an alternative UoM in a purchasing info record?

entry.title: Re: In MRP Live can you run MRP as quick as MD03 in Class...
entry.content:
Hello @MRO-Tim-McLain 

You can link MD01N (MRP Live) to MD04 with a navigation profile definition - SPRO Production -> Material Requirements Planning -> Evaluation -> Define Navigation Profiles, e.g.

Then MD01N button is visible in MD04 with the navigation profile SAPPPMRP00:

and MD01N is called when the button is pushed. The material number and the plant are transferred to the MD01N selection screen.

Of course you can use a custom navigation profile if you don't want to change the standard SAPPPMRP00.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: IPS Job finished with error: error=The request to create an entity has failed with: 409 Conflict 'A
entry.content:
Dear all, 

 

Dear all, I have the following use case: I ran an IPS job, and it finished with an error. The reason is that a user was already created before the job ran. Is there a solution to avoid this problem? I discovered that I need to delete the user: 2907578 - Failed or missing users during IPS provisioning - User profile with email [<name>@<domain>] already exists | SAP Knowledge Base Article

Can the IPS job recognize the user and simply update it?

Thanks for your support.

entry.title: Configure SAP CPI Mail Adapter with Outlook SMTP using OAuth2 Authentication
entry.content:
1. Introduction
This guide provides a detailed step-by-step procedure to configure the SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) Mail Adapter to send emails using Outlook SMTP with OAuth2 authentication.
2. Prerequisites
Before proceeding, ensure you have the following:
• Access to an SAP CPI tenant
• Outlook account with access to Azure AD (Azure
Active Directory)
• Administrator privileges in Azure AD
• Client ID and Client Secret for OAuth2
authentication
• Access to the SAP CPI Web UI

3. Create a New App Registration in Azure AD
• Step 1: Register a new app in Azure Active
Directory.

  • Go to the Azure Portal: portal.azure.com.

Scroll down and find App resistration -

Navigate to Azure Active Directory > App Registrations

> New Registration.

 

Click on App resistration -

Create app > Fill details -

Fill in the following fields:

  • Name: Enter a name for the app (e.g., "SAP CPI Mail Adapter").
  • Supported account types: Select Accounts in this organizational directory only.
  • Redirect URI: Set the URI to

https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient (or another relevant URI as needed).

Click Register.

App is Created –

Step 2: Get the Client ID and Tenant ID

  1. After the registration, copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID. These are required for authentication.

     

    Step 3: Generate a Client Secret
  2. Go to Certificates & Secrets in the app registration.
  3. Under Client secrets, click New client secret.
  4. Add a description (e.g., "SAP CPI Mail Secret") and set the expiration.
  5. Click Add and copy the Value of the client secret. This is your Client Secret.

Create New +

Copy Secret value it will be used at the time of creating OAuth 2 Authentication Code configuration

5.   Configure SAP CPI Mail Adapter

Step 1: Navigate to the SAP CPI Web UI

  1. Go to the SAP CPI tenant.
  2. Open the Integration Flow (iFlow) that you want to configure for sending emails.

Step : Set up Mail Adapter

  1. Add the Mail Adapter to your iFlow.
  2. In the Mail Adapter settings:
    • Set SMTP Server to smtp.office365.com.
    • Set Port to 587.
    • Set Security to STARTTLS.

Step 3: Configure OAuth2 Authentication

  1. In the Authentication Type dropdown, select OAuth2.
  2. Configure the OAuth2 Authorization Code Credential with the following details:
    • Authorization URL:

https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize

 

  • Token URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token

 

  • Client ID: Enter the Application (client) ID from the Azure AD registration.

 

  • Client Secret: Enter the Client Secret you generated in Azure AD.

 

  • Scope:

Enter https://graph.microsoft.com/.default.

 

  • https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.Send ofline_access

 

Fill all fileds –

Grant Permissions: Ensure that the Outlook account has the necessary permissions to send emails (via Mail.Send).

Add These permissions-

6.   Troubleshooting

Common Errors and Solutions

  • Error: AADSTS65001: The user or administrator has not consented to use the application
    • Solution: Ensure that the admin consent is granted in Azure AD.
  • Error: SendAsDeniedException
    • Solution: Ensure that the correct account is being used to send emails and that permissions are set correctly in Azure AD.

7.   Conclusion

  • Now you have successfully configured the SAP CPI Mail Adapter with Outlook SMTP using OAuth2 authentication. This setup enables secure email communication through the Outlook service, using the recommended OAuth2 protocol.

Appendix

  • Important URLs:
    • Azure AD Portal
    • SAP CPI Documentation
    • Microsoft Graph API

NOTE –

These Can be done with only Administrative account of MS 365 only and for both accounts Business Account (@company.com or @School.com) and Personal Account (@outlook.com) settings are different!

Check Authentication for the Email -

 For Personal account if you are a free user then you have to change it manually after this from manifest !

 

Thanks,
Dushyant

entry.title: Re: Custom Consumption Complex CDS view to Enable the CDC
entry.content: Check out the following SAP blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/cds-based-data-extraction-part-ii-delta-handling/ba-p/13425761

entry.title: Re: ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI - DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR
entry.content: The Note: 2810832 - Condition save occurring twice when using ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN is already implemented in our system and does not resolve the issue, since it is the one that add those extra checks on call stack before the RV_CONDITION_SAVE.

entry.title: 📅 Day 3/30 – Master Data Essentials in SAP PP 🧩📚🏭
entry.content:
In SAP PP, master data is the foundation upon which all planning, execution, and control processes are built. Just like you can’t build a house without bricks, you can’t run efficient production without properly maintained master data! 🏗️💡

Here's a quick overview of the core master data objects in SAP PP:

🔹 📦 Material Master
The material master contains information on all the materials your company produces, procures, stores, or sells. It impacts everything — from production to purchasing and inventory.

🔹 📘 Bill of Materials (BOM)
The BOM is a structured list of components needed to manufacture a product. Think of it as a recipe for manufacturing — including materials, subassemblies, and quantities.

🔹 ⚙️ Work Centers
These represent the physical locations where production operations are performed (e.g. machines, assembly lines). Work centers include key data such as capacity, scheduling formulas, and cost centers.

🔹 📋 Routings
Routings define the sequence of operations required to produce a material. They are essential for planning lead times, capacity, and costing.

✅ Pro Tip:
Accurate and consistent master data is crucial for successful MRP runs, production orders, and cost calculations. Invest time in setting it up right — your downstream processes will thank you! 🔍✅

💬 Do you manage master data in SAP? What’s the most challenging part in your experience — BOM changes, routing accuracy, or material classification?

👇 Let me know in the comments!

entry.title: Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Background:

Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) in SAP S/4HANA refers to the financial responsibility a company needs to restore or retire an asset at the end of its useful life, often due to legal or contractual requirements. This obligation is typically recorded as a liability in financial statements and is accounted for under various accounting standards, such as US GAAP and IFRS.

 

In this article let's try to understand the ARO process in it's lifecycle, what are the master data, transactional data objects involved and how each process are connected to asset accounting and General Ledger, specifically on the accounting principles and ledgers for respective accounting standards.

In SAP S/4HANA, ARO is managed through structured processes that include:
- ARO Configuration: Setting up the integration between ARO and fixed asset accounting.
- ARO Creation and Release: Defining asset classes, cost objects, and contract durations.
- Accretion Posting: Monthly accruals to reflect the increasing liability over time.
- Depreciation Charges: Calculating depreciation using methods like straight-line depreciation.
- Settlement/Decommissioning: Finalizing the obligation by recording the actual costs incurred.

 

I will few more articles in future which covers specific aspects about BC Sets to import initial set up, Configuration steps, migration of ARO Simple approach and detailed approach.

entry.title: Re: A Relationship (basic) of MM and FI tables
entry.content:
Hello Jao,

It's so helpful to understand the basic integration of MM/FI, thanks a lot for sharing.

Regards,

Chafik

entry.title: 🐞 SAP SuccessFactors Bug Report: Duplicate Transactions Triggered by the Escape Key
entry.content:
📌Overview

SAP SuccessFactors is one of the most robust and widely used cloud-based HCM (Human Capital Management) suites in the market today. Its modular design, including Employee Central, Time Management, Recruiting, and Performance Management, allows businesses to manage their workforce efficiently. These modules are leveraged by thousands of organizations globally to manage day-to-day HR operations.

Given this extensive reach and the business-critical nature of the application, even low-severity bugs can have widespread implications. Ensuring a seamless, intuitive experience for end-users is essential for productivity and system trust.

Recently, a low-level yet recurring issue has been observed in commonly used transactions related to time and employee data management:

  • View Time Sheet for [Employee]

  • Administer Time for [Employee]

  • View Job Information for [Employee]

  • View Name for [Employee]

  • View Employment Information for [Employee]

     

    ⚠️The Bug: Duplicate Transactions When Pressing ESC Key

    When performing tasks in View Time Sheet or Administer Time, pressing the Escape (ESC) key — a standard user behavior intended to clear transaction — triggers a duplicate instance of the transaction

    🔄Steps to Reproduce:

    1. Log in to SAP SuccessFactors.

    2. Navigate to either Administer Time or View Time Sheet.

    3. Press Escape Key After entering the Transaction

    4. Result: The same transaction is re-invoked automatically

    ‌‌Call to Action for SAP Product Team

    Given the nature of this issue and its presence in key transactional areas, it is imperative that the SAP SuccessFactors product team evaluates and addresses this behavior as part of their ongoing commitment to usability and system stability. Even minor inconsistencies in expected user interactions—such as the misuse of the ESC key—can erode user confidence and introduce inefficiencies at scale. We recommend this be flagged for review and inclusion in a future maintenance release or patch cycle.

  •  

    Screenshots of the Bug:

entry.title: Re: How to Monitor Adobe Document Services (ADS) Request...
entry.content:
Hi Park,

Thanks for the note, but I'm actually looking for a tool that works specifically in the Public Edition environment.

entry.title: HTTP 431 Error – Work Zone Admin UI Not Accessible Due to Request Header Size
entry.content:
We are experiencing a consistent 431 Request Header Fields Too Large error when accessing the SAP Build Work Zone (standard edition) Admin UI in our development subaccount.

  • The base domain returns 302 Found, redirecting to /sites

  • The 431 only happens when the browser sends a Cookie header (e.g., user session or SSO)

  • The backend CAP service is working and reachable via direct app URLs

  • The HTML5 application is deployed correctly via the Managed AppRouter

  • The issue persists across browsers and profiles, and also occurs in curl requests with cookies

entry.title: VAT on down-payments made in Public cloud
entry.content: The client requests the process to post down payment made to supplier with VAT amount. I don't see where to configure "VAT on down-payments made" G/L account. Could you advise this process in Public Cloud? Thank you.

entry.title: hide pay component section in the compensation info bloc
entry.content:
Hello experts,

In the compensation info bloc, the client wants only to show specific fields about benefits such as "eligible for car", eligible for bonus... Is it possible to hide reccruring pay component and pay target sub-sections?

entry.title: Re: How to Monitor Adobe Document Services (ADS) Request...
entry.content: oh public not PCE Sorry

entry.title: Re: How to improve Date Dimension labels in a line chart...
entry.content: Use the standard time dimensions (incl. hierarchies): https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/c5cfce4d22b04650b2fd6078762cdeb9.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Is it possible to refer the SAP BTP standard roles in xs-security.json?
entry.content:
Is it possible to refer the SAP BTP standard roles in xs-security.json? We have set-up role collections manually referencing the standard role, however we are unable to refer standard role in xs-security.json. Every blog/github code is creating role template and then role collection referencing that collection. However I am trying to refer SAP roles as this will save lot of time. 
Can someone please confirm if referencing SAP standard roles or role collections in xs-security.json is possible or not? If possible can someone share the code snippet how to refer the SAP standard roles or role collections in xs-security.json?

entry.title: Unable to define Custom Naming Rules for Payment Medium Files
entry.content:
I want to define a Custom Naming Rules for the Payment Medium File format.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to define Custom Naming Rules for Payment Medi...
entry.content:
DME file naming convention, file names are fix, they are generated automatically by the system hence they cannot be changed. Possible suggestion is download the file, open it and save it with the required name convention that your bank requires.

As described in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Help Portal: Defining Custom Naming Rules for Payment Medium Files:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/c3bf972389694cc5bb91ba78a85fc83e.html?q=Japan
